content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Updated the doc comment on rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs) to explicitly state that it relies on append-only chronological ordering of the journal rather than incorrectly claiming it keeps the 'newest recorded_at' row via timestamp comparison. The code uses unconditional HashMap::insert which is only correct because journal events are append-only; the doc comment now makes this invariant explicit. Amendment 2: Changed FileSystem::live_project_root to FileSystem::project_root in record_planned_elsewhere_amendments (engine.rs), so the prompt is loaded using the same fallback-aware path resolution used elsewhere in the engine. project_root tries the live workspace first, then falls back to the audit workspace, so audit-only/legacy workspaces will no longer fail the prompt read and silently produce an empty allowed-id set that drops all PE mappings. Amendment 3: Added run/round supersession logic to rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs). After the identity-based collapse, for each (active_bead_id, run_id) group where run_id is Some, the maximum completion_round is determined and only mappings from that round are kept. Earlier rounds' PE decisions that were later fixed or remapped are now filtered out at the persistence/read layer, not just ad-hoc in success_reconciliation.rs. Legacy mappings (run_id=None or completion_round=None) are kept unconditionally since they lack provenance for round filtering.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Updated doc comment on rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs) from 'keeping the latest (newest recorded_at) row for each key' to 'keeping the last journal entry for each key (relies on append-only chronological ordering of the journal)', making the append-only invariant explicit rather than incorrectly implying timestamp-based comparison.
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: Changed FileSystem::live_project_root(base_dir, project_id) to FileSystem::project_root(base_dir, project_id) in record_planned_elsewhere_amendments (engine.rs line 7618). project_root tries live_project_root first, then falls back to audit_project_root, matching the resolution pattern used by the engine's project_root_path helper.
3. [Completed] Amendment 3: Added run/round supersession after the identity-based collapse in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs). Builds a HashMap<(active_bead_id, run_id), u32> tracking the max completion_round per group, then filters collapsed mappings to only retain those from the max round for their group. Mappings with run_id=None or completion_round=None are kept unconditionally.
4. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (lib, integration, unit, doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (service.rs, engine.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: doc comment at service.rs:2438-2443 now reads 'keeping the last journal entry for each key (relies on append-only chronological ordering of the journal)' instead of the misleading 'keeping the latest (newest recorded_at) row'
- Amendment 2 verified: engine.rs line 7618 now uses FileSystem::project_root which falls back to audit_project_root when live_project_root does not exist, matching the engine's project_root_path helper at line 6583-6584
- Amendment 3 verified: rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal now applies round-based supersession after identity collapse. For each (active_bead_id, run_id) group with Some run_id, only mappings from the max completion_round survive. The existing service.rs tests pass because test mappings use run_id=None and completion_round=None (legacy format), which bypass the filter unconditionally.
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 2 files changed, 37 insertions, 5 deletions (service.rs +35/-3, engine.rs +2/-2)
- Files modified: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs (doc comment fix + round supersession), src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs (project_root fallback)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The round-based supersession in rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal and the ad-hoc round filter in success_reconciliation.rs:744-753 now overlap in function. The success_reconciliation filter remains as defense-in-depth but is now redundant for mappings returned by load_planned_elsewhere_mappings. Removing it would be a minor cleanup but is left for a future round to avoid unnecessary churn.
- The round supersession groups by (active_bead_id, run_id). If two different runs produce PE mappings for the same bead, each run's mappings are independently filtered to their own max round. Cross-run filtering relies on the exact run_id match in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success.
- Legacy mappings without run_id or completion_round bypass round supersession entirely. This is by design — these mappings predate the provenance fields and cannot be safely filtered. Over time, as old journal entries age out, this gap narrows.
